items.headline: Geylang 4-room HDB flat sells for $1.1M, setting a new area record
items.summary: A 4-room HDB flat in Geylang changed hands at $1.1 million, a new high for the estate, even though it sits on a lower floor where prices are typically softer.
items.meaning: Even a historically lower-priced HDB town is now crossing the million-dollar mark, which shows resale demand and prices are broadly elevated across Singapore, not just in prime areas.
items.body:
A 4-room HDB flat in Geylang has sold for $1.1 million, making it the most expensive resale deal ever recorded in that estate. What makes this stand out is that the unit is on a lower floor, where prices usually sit softer than mid or high floors.

Geylang has historically been one of Singapore's more affordable HDB towns. It is not Bishan or Queenstown. The fact that even a lower-floor flat there has crossed the million-dollar mark shows how broadly the resale price rally has spread, well beyond the estates buyers traditionally think of as premium.

For sellers around Singapore, this is a signal that buyer demand is strong enough to push prices into record territory in areas that were cheap just a few years ago. For buyers, the message is straightforward: do not assume a less fashionable address means an affordable price. Budget carefully and check recent transaction data before assuming what you can afford.
items.headline_zh: 芽笼4房式组屋以$1.1M成交，刷新该区转售记录
items.summary_zh: 芽笼一个4房式组屋以$1.1M转手，创下该区历史最高转售价。值得注意的是，这个单位位于较低楼层，而低楼层价格通常较为偏软。
items.meaning_zh: 就连历来价格偏低的HDB市镇，如今也跨过了百万元门槛，显示全岛转售需求和房价都普遍走高，不只是黄金地段才如此。
items.body_zh:
芽笼一个4房式组屋以$1.1 million成交，成为该区有史以来最贵的转售交易。特别之处在于，这个单位位于较低楼层，低楼层单位的价格通常比中层或高层来得软。

芽笼历来是新加坡较实惠的HDB市镇之一，比不上碧山或女皇镇。但就连这里的低楼层单位也突破百万元，说明转售价格的涨势已扩散得多广，远不只是买家传统上认为的优质市镇。

对全岛各地的卖家来说，这是一个信号：买家的需求足够强劲，就连几年前还很便宜的地区，也能把价格推至破纪录的水平。对买家而言，信息很直接：别以为地段不那么抢手，价格就一定实惠。做好预算规划，出手前先查近期成交数据，弄清楚自己真正负担得起的范围。

items.headline: HDB pilot will let licensed cat fosterers keep up to 6 cats per flat
items.summary: Under a new pilot scheme, HDB residents who are licensed cat fosterers can house up to six cats in their flat, well above the usual one-cat-per-flat rule.
items.meaning: If you are an HDB owner and an active cat fosterer, this pilot opens up more flexibility, though conditions apply and this is not a blanket change for all residents.
items.stat_label: cats allowed per flat in pilot
items.body:
HDB has started a pilot scheme that allows residents who are licensed cat fosterers to keep up to six cats in their flat. That is a significant step up from the current rule, which limits all HDB households to one cat.

The key detail is the word licensed. This is not a change for the average cat owner who just has a couple of cats at home. To qualify, you need to be an active fosterer registered with a recognised welfare organisation, and HDB will set conditions on how the arrangement works. The scheme exists to make life easier for people who temporarily house rescued cats or cats waiting for adoption.

If you are already doing cat fostering work from an HDB flat, this is worth looking into because it removes a practical constraint that has likely created friction for you. For everyone else, the one-cat rule stands. This pilot does not change anything for the general HDB population.
items.headline_zh: HDB试行计划：持牌猫咪寄养者每户最多可养6只猫
items.summary_zh: 根据新试行计划，持牌猫咪寄养者可在HDB单位内饲养多达6只猫，远高于目前每户限养1只的规定。
items.meaning_zh: 如果你是HDB屋主，同时也是活跃的猫咪寄养者，这个试行计划给你带来更大的灵活性，不过有条件限制，并非对所有居民一概放宽。
items.stat_label_zh: 试行计划下每户可饲养猫咪数量
items.body_zh:
HDB推出了一项试行计划，允许持牌猫咪寄养者在组屋单位内饲养多达6只猫。这比目前每户只能养1只猫的规定，迈进了一大步。

关键词是「持牌」。这不是针对在家随便养了几只猫的普通猫主人的改变。要符合资格，你必须是在认可动物福利机构注册的活跃寄养者，HDB也会对具体安排设定条件。这个计划的出发点，是为那些临时照顾获救猫咪或等待领养的猫咪的人减少不便。

如果你本来就在HDB组屋里做猫咪寄养工作，这个计划值得关注，因为它解决了一个在实际操作上给你带来不少麻烦的限制。对其他人来说，每户1只猫的规定依然不变。这个试行计划不影响一般HDB居民。

items.headline: Most buyers of million-dollar HDB resale flats in 2025 paid nothing above bank valuation
items.summary: PropNex data shows that most people who bought HDB resale flats at $1 million or more in 2025 paid no cash-over-valuation, meaning banks appraised these flats at or close to what buyers paid.
items.meaning: If you are stretching to buy a high-priced resale HDB flat, there is a reasonable chance your bank will value it near the asking price, so you may not need extra cash on top of your loan.
items.stat_label: resales where buyers paid no COV
items.body:
Data from PropNex shows that most buyers who purchased HDB resale flats at $1 million or above in 2025 paid no cash-over-valuation. That means the banks appraised these flats at or very close to what buyers actually paid, with little or no gap between the two figures.

Cash-over-valuation, or COV, is the amount a buyer pays on top of the bank's assessed value, and it must come entirely from cash rather than CPF or a loan. In previous hot markets, COV sometimes ran into tens of thousands of dollars and caught buyers off guard. The fact that it is largely absent at the million-dollar level is genuinely useful information for anyone planning a purchase at that price range.

If you are stretching to buy a high-priced resale flat, this suggests the bank valuation is less likely to come in short and leave you scrambling for extra cash. That said, it is still smart to review recent comparable transactions before making an offer, so you understand what the bank is likely to accept as a reasonable value.
items.headline_zh: 2025年，大多数百万组屋转售买家无需支付COV
items.summary_zh: PropNex数据显示，2025年大多数以$1M或以上购入HDB转售单位的买家没有支付现金超额估价（COV），即银行估值与买家实际付出的价格相当接近。
items.meaning_zh: 如果你打算买高价转售HDB单位，银行估值与开价相近的可能性不低，意味着你或许不需要额外准备一笔现金垫付。
items.body_zh:
PropNex的数据显示，2025年以$1 million或以上购入HDB转售单位的买家，大多数都没有支付现金超额估价（COV）。也就是说，银行对这些单位的估值，与买家实际支付的价格相当接近，两者之间差距很小。

现金超额估价，也就是COV，指的是买家在银行估值基础上额外支付的金额，这笔钱必须全部用现金支付，不能动用CPF或贷款。在过去楼市火热的时期，COV有时高达数万元，让买家措手不及。百万元级别的单位基本上不需要支付COV，这对于有意在这个价位购房的人来说，是非常实用的信息。

如果你正努力凑够买高价转售单位的钱，这说明银行估值短缺、让你手忙脚乱的情况发生概率较低。话虽如此，在出价之前，还是应该查看近期同类成交记录，了解银行大概会接受的合理估值范围。

items.headline: HDB and condo rentals both pick up in June after a slow May
items.summary: Leasing activity for both HDB flats and private condos recovered in June, reversing the lull in May that tends to happen during the school holiday period.
items.meaning: If you are a landlord or a tenant looking for a place, the market is moving again with more listings and inquiries than a month ago.
items.body:
Rental activity for both HDB flats and private condos picked up in June after a quieter May. The dip in May is a pattern that repeats most years, as school holidays temporarily reduce the number of tenants actively looking to move.

With school back in session and companies bringing in new hires ahead of the second half of the year, the market typically sees a lift in June and July. This year is following that same rhythm, with more listings appearing and more inquiries coming through compared to a month ago.

For landlords who held off listing during the quiet period, now is a reasonable time to put your unit on the market. For tenants currently hunting for a home, there are more options to choose from than there were in May, though that also means more competition from other renters looking at the same units.
items.headline_zh: 6月份HDB和condo租赁活动双双回升，走出5月淡季
items.summary_zh: HDB单位和私人condo的租赁活动在6月份回升，扭转了5月学校假期期间惯常出现的淡静局面。
items.meaning_zh: 如果你是房东或正在找房的租客，市场已重新活跃，盘源和询问量都比上个月多。
items.body_zh:
HDB单位和私人condo的租赁活动在6月份重新活跃，走出了5月的淡静期。5月较为清淡是每年都会出现的规律，因为学校假期期间，主动寻找住所的租客会暂时减少。

随着学校复课，加上各公司为下半年引进新员工，市场在6月和7月通常会迎来一波活跃期。今年也不例外，与上个月相比，盘源数量和询问量都有所增加。

对于在淡静期按兵不动的房东来说，现在是把单位推出市场的合适时机。对于目前正在找房的租客，可选的房源比5月份多了，不过这也意味着，同时在看同一个单位的竞争者也多了。

items.headline: Old Tiong Bahru condo averaged $306K profit per 2-bedroom unit, beating newer projects nearby
items.summary: Resale data shows a long-standing Tiong Bahru condo delivered average profits of $306,000 per 2-bedroom unit, outperforming newer developments in Bukit Merah and Queenstown on the same metric.
items.meaning: Older, well-located condos can beat flashier new launches on resale returns, so do not write them off just because of their age when comparing investment potential.
items.body:
Resale figures for a long-running condo in Tiong Bahru show it delivered average profits of $306,000 per 2-bedroom unit sold, a figure that beats what owners of newer developments in Bukit Merah and Queenstown made on the same flat type over a comparable period.

The result reflects what many who know the area already understand. Tiong Bahru has a loyal buyer base, a distinct neighbourhood identity, and limited new supply. Those factors keep prices firm even as the buildings age, and they proved more valuable than the newer finishes offered by more recently built projects nearby.

The broader point for anyone comparing investment potential is that age is not a reliable proxy for returns. A well-located older condo in a neighbourhood with genuine demand can outperform a newer launch just down the road. Before discounting an older project because of its age or en-bloc uncertainty, look at what past sellers actually walked away with.
items.headline_zh: 中峇鲁老牌condo每个2房式单位平均获利$306K，超越附近较新项目
items.summary_zh: 转售数据显示，中峇鲁一个历史悠久的condo每个2房式单位平均获利$306,000，在同类比较中跑赢红山和女皇镇的较新发展项目。
items.meaning_zh: 地点优越的老牌condo，在转售回报方面可以胜过看起来更新更亮眼的新项目。比较投资潜力时，不要单凭楼龄就把它们排除在外。
items.body_zh:
中峇鲁一个历史悠久的condo的转售数字显示，每个已售出的2房式单位平均获利$306,000，在同类比较下，这个数字超过了红山和女皇镇较新发展项目的屋主，在相近期间内从同类单位取得的回报。

这个结果反映了熟悉该区的人早就明白的道理。中峇鲁有一批忠实的买家群体、独特的街区气质，以及有限的新供应量。这些因素让价格在楼龄增长的同时保持坚挺，事实证明，这比附近较新项目提供的现代装潢更有价值。

对于任何在比较投资潜力的人来说，更宽泛的启示在于：楼龄不是判断回报的可靠指标。一个地点优越、需求真实的老牌condo，可能表现胜过隔条街才推出的新项目。在因为楼龄或en bloc的不确定性而否定一个老项目之前，先看看过去的卖家实际上入袋了多少。

items.headline: New condo sales fell 65% in June to a two-year low as launches dried up
items.summary: Developer sales of new private homes dropped 65% in June, hitting the lowest monthly figure in two years, mainly because very few projects were launched during the school holiday period.
items.meaning: The sharp fall is mostly a supply issue and not a signal of weak demand, so a rebound is expected in July as more launches come to market.
items.stat_label: drop in new condo sales in june
items.body:
Developer sales of new private homes in Singapore dropped 65% in June, falling to the lowest monthly total in two years. The sharp decline came mainly because very few new projects were launched during the month, which tracks with the usual pattern of developers avoiding June school holidays for major releases.

This is a supply story, not a demand story. The buyers are still out there. Projects that launched earlier in the year sold well, and there is no evidence that buyers have suddenly lost interest. The low June number simply reflects a near-empty pipeline of new units for the month.

A rebound in July and August is the most likely outcome as developers bring more launches to market. If you are tracking new launches before making a buying decision, do not read the June figures as a sign the market is cooling. It is more a calendar gap than a change in underlying conditions.
items.headline_zh: 6月新condo销量骤跌65%，创两年低点，新推项目寥寥无几
items.summary_zh: 6月份发展商新私宅销量下跌65%，创下两年来的单月最低纪录，主要原因是学校假期期间几乎没有新项目推出。
items.meaning_zh: 销量大跌主要是供应问题，不是需求疲软的信号。随着更多项目在7月推出，预计会出现反弹。
items.body_zh:
新加坡6月份发展商新私宅销量下跌65%，跌至两年来的单月最低水平。销量大跌的主要原因，是当月几乎没有新项目推出，这与发展商惯常避开6月学校假期进行大型新盘发布的规律一致。

这是一个供应的故事，不是需求的故事。买家依然在场。今年早些时候推出的项目销情良好，没有任何迹象显示买家突然失去了兴趣。6月份的低数字，只是反映了当月新单位供应近乎断层的情况。

7月和8月最有可能出现反弹，届时发展商会陆续推出更多新项目。如果你在追踪新盘发布再决定是否入市，不要把6月的数字解读为市场降温的信号。这更像是日历上的一个空档，而非市场基本面的转变。

items.headline: Bayshore Drive site draws record $2.13B bid, the highest ever for a non-CBD government land plot
items.summary: A Frasers Property-led consortium won the Bayshore Drive mixed-use site with a $2.13 billion bid, setting the highest price ever paid for a government land sale site outside the central business district.
items.meaning: The record land cost will likely be passed on to future buyers when this site launches as condos, so expect above-average prices for the Bayshore area.
items.body:
A consortium led by Frasers Property won the government tender for a mixed-use site on Bayshore Drive with a bid of $2.13 billion, the highest price ever paid for a government land sale plot outside the central business district. The site sits near the upcoming Bayshore MRT station in the East Coast area and is expected to be developed into a mix of homes and commercial space.

At $2.13 billion for the land alone, the numbers set a clear floor for what the developer needs to recover. Construction costs, financing and margin all sit on top of that. Record land prices like this almost always translate into above-average launch prices when the units eventually go on sale to the public.

If you are planning to buy in the Bayshore area, use this as a reference point when weighing new launches against older resale options nearby. The homes coming from this site are unlikely to be priced modestly. For existing owners in the East Coast corridor, a flagship development of this scale and price tends to support broader prices in the neighbourhood over time.
items.headline_zh: Bayshore Drive地段收获$2.13B竞标价，创非CBD政府售地历史最高纪录
items.summary_zh: 由Frasers Property牵头的财团以$2.13B的出价赢得Bayshore Drive综合发展地段，创下中央商业区以外政府售地有史以来的最高价。
items.meaning_zh: 破纪录的地价很可能会转嫁给未来的买家，届时这个地段推出condo时，预计价格将高于Bayshore一带的平均水平。
items.stat_label_zh: 非CBD政府售地最高竞标价
items.body_zh:
由Frasers Property牵头的财团以$2.13 billion的竞标价，赢得了Bayshore Drive综合发展地段的政府招标，创下中央商业区以外政府售地的历史最高价。该地段毗邻即将开通的Bayshore MRT站，位于东海岸一带，预计将发展为住宅与商业空间混合的项目。

单是地价已达$2.13 billion，这个数字为发展商需要回收的成本设定了一个清晰的底线。建筑费用、融资成本和利润都叠加在这之上。这样的破纪录地价，几乎必然会转化为单位上市时高于平均水平的售价。

如果你有意在Bayshore一带购房，在权衡新推项目与附近二手房选项时，可以把这个数字作为参考。从这个地段推出的新房，价格不太可能会便宜。对于东海岸一带的现有屋主来说，这种规模和价位的旗舰发展项目，往往能在长期内支撑整个区域的房价。

items.headline: Queenstown 2-room HDB flat sells for $696K, a new national record
items.summary: A 2-room flat in Queenstown sold for $696,000, setting a new all-time high for that flat type anywhere in Singapore.
items.meaning: Even the smallest HDB flat type is hitting record prices in mature central estates, showing just how tight supply is in well-located areas.
items.body:
A 2-room HDB flat in Queenstown has sold for $696,000, the highest price ever recorded for that flat type in Singapore. The transaction sets a new national benchmark and underscores how far prices have moved even at the smallest end of the public housing market.

Two-room flats are often thought of as entry-level or investment units, so a sale approaching $700,000 for one in Queenstown reflects how scarce well-located HDB stock has become. Queenstown's proximity to the city centre, its established amenities, and the limited availability of units in mature estates all push prices toward levels that would have seemed implausible not long ago.

For owners of 2-room flats in mature central estates, this sale shows the ceiling on your asset is higher than many assumed. For buyers, it drives home the premium attached to location in Singapore's HDB market regardless of flat size. If you want a central address and are working within a tight budget, the trade-off between size and where you live has never been more stark.
items.headline_zh: 女皇镇2房式组屋以$696K成交，创全国最高纪录
items.summary_zh: 女皇镇一个2房式组屋以$696,000成交，创下全新加坡该类型单位的历史最高价。
items.meaning_zh: 就连最小的HDB单位类型，在成熟的中部市镇也在创下价格新高，足见地段优越地区的供应有多紧张。
items.body_zh:
女皇镇一个2房式组屋以$696,000成交，创下全新加坡该类型单位的历史最高价。这笔交易树立了全国新标杆，凸显了即便是公共住房市场中最小的户型，价格也已走出了多远。

2房式单位通常被视为入门级或投资型单位，因此女皇镇一个接近$700,000的售价，反映出地段优越的组屋存量有多稀缺。女皇镇靠近市区、配套设施成熟，加上成熟市镇的单位供应有限，种种因素把价格推向了在不久前还难以想象的水平。

对于成熟中部市镇2房式单位的屋主来说，这笔交易表明，你手上资产的上限比很多人预想的还要高。对买家而言，这再次印证了在新加坡的HDB市场，不论单位大小，地段溢价都实实在在地存在。如果你希望住在市区地段，但预算又相当有限，大小与地段之间的取舍，从来没有像现在这样明显。
